steps.title: Rearrange into standard quadratic form
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side and combine like terms. Starting with $nx^2 - 16x = 26x^2 - 8$:
$$nx^2 - 26x^2 - 16x + 8 = 0$$
$$(n - 26)x^2 - 16x + 8 = 0$$

steps.title: Apply discriminant condition for two distinct solutions
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ must be positive. With $a = n - 26$, $b = -16$, $c = 8$:
$$(-16)^2 - 4(n - 26)(8) > 0$$
$$256 - 32(n - 26) > 0$$
$$256 - 32n + 832 > 0$$
$$1088 - 32n > 0$$
$$n < 34$$
The greatest integer $n$ satisfying this condition is 33.
